dimensions.label: Native total context window
dimensions.definition: The total input-plus-output window documented for the exact model backend.

dimensions.label: Native maximum input
dimensions.definition: The maximum input accepted by the exact model backend before client and hidden overhead.

dimensions.label: Native maximum output
dimensions.definition: The maximum output reserved by the exact model backend.

dimensions.label: Effective Codex budget
dimensions.definition: The total budget Codex exposes after its client reserve; this is not maximum user input.
dimensions.authority: Resolved Codex runtime configuration and live probe

dimensions.label: Operational input threshold
dimensions.definition: The calibrated checkpoint and rollover threshold after instructions, tools, and output allowance.

dimensions.label: Durable reservoir capacity
dimensions.definition: Tokenizer-counted context retained in the local integrity-checked store outside the active model window.

public_claims.text: GPT-5.6 Sol with its native 1.05M window. Compaction blocked. Durable continuity beyond the window.
Every compliant task uses exact gpt-5.6-sol with no model fallback.
The active GPT-5.6 Sol backend exposes its documented 1,050,000-token total context window.
GPT-5.6 Sol has a separate 922,000-token maximum input.
GPT-5.6 Sol has a separate 128,000-token maximum output.
Codex resolves at least a 1,000,000-token internal budget while the operating threshold remains below maximum input.
The local reservoir retains at least 1,000,000 tokenizer-counted tokens with exact retrieval and integrity evidence.
Known automatic and manual compaction attempts are checkpointed and visibly blocked.
Every fresh supported Codex task is automatically enrolled or visibly blocked before ordinary work.
Ordinary storage and retrieval are local-only and telemetry-off by default.
The supported release installs from verified GitHub artifacts rather than a developer checkout.

limitations.text: The 1,050,000-token total window is not a claim of one million input tokens.
A catalog value or status screen is not native-window proof without a live above-cap Sol request.
Durable reservoir capacity cannot substitute for the active native model window.
Blocking PreCompact can halt the active turn at its hard limit.
Successor rollover continues work in a verified fresh task, not an infinitely live source task.
The CAC-01 runtime-resolved Sol catalog is 272,000 total with 258,400 effective tokens.
Sol requests above 272,000 input tokens receive documented premium pricing and require a cost gate.
